title: Add separate CLI smoke workflow with runtime metrics
description:
Context:
We need end-to-end regression confidence without bloating the default fast pipeline. This issue owns the separate smoke workflow that runs the decoding CLI over the representative fixture corpus, checks the locked expected outputs, and publishes lightweight runtime metrics.

Deliverables:
- workflow/job that runs the decoding CLI over the representative archaeology fixture corpus
- verification of exit-code behavior and output drift against the locked expected outputs
- elapsed-time and output-size metrics emitted as a machine-readable artifact
- workflow separation that keeps the fast quality gate lean
acceptance_criteria:
1. The smoke workflow runs the decoding CLI over the representative fixture corpus and fails on drift from the locked expected outputs.
2. The smoke workflow verifies the declared Phase 1 exit-code behavior on both converged and escalated fixtures.
3. The workflow emits elapsed wall-clock time and output-size metrics as a machine-readable artifact for trend monitoring.
4. The smoke path is kept out of the fast quality-gate workflow so default pipeline runtime stays bounded.
notes:
Target files:
- .github/workflows/smoke.yml
- tests/smoke.rs

Reservation guidance: own smoke automation and metric capture only; do not weaken assertions or move smoke into the fast CI path.

title: Emit priority_break when source_priority decides liveness
description: Bug hunt follow-up: resolver currently wires source_priority for liveness selection but never emits resolution_kind=priority_break, leaving one frozen Phase 1 resolution_kind unreachable. Add a focused resolver regression and set priority_break when source_priority actually selects among compatible liveness groups by rank.

title: Implement shared archaeology fixture loaders
description:
Context:
Raw fixture assets should be authored separately from support code. This issue owns only the reusable loader/helpers that turn the shared archaeology corpus into typed claim and policy inputs for tests.

Deliverables:
- shared helpers for loading claim JSONL fixtures
- shared helpers for loading policy fixtures
- shared helpers for locating expected-output fixtures without ad hoc per-test path logic
- no new raw fixture corpus assets, snapshot assertions, or CI workflow code
acceptance_criteria:
1. Shared fixture helpers can load claim and policy fixtures without ad hoc per-test parsing.
2. Helper APIs are reusable across contract, resolver, render, report, and CLI tests.
3. The loader layer does not invent new fixture data or embed snapshot assertions.
4. This task stays limited to support code and does not own smoke workflow wiring.
notes:
Target files:
- tests/support/mod.rs
- tests/support/fixtures.rs

Reservation guidance: own reusable fixture-loading support code only; raw fixtures live in the dedicated corpus bead.

title: Reject unknown fields in frozen output contracts
description: The frozen output contracts (, , ) currently deserialize permissively because the top-level structs and nested objects do not use . That masks schema drift and weakens the Phase 1 frozen-wire guarantee. Add strict deserialization coverage and reject unknown fields on output contract types.

title: Do not label output write failures as refusal
description: CLI currently reports output creation/write failures as refusal status even though refusal is reserved for contract violations. Track a focused fix in src/cli.rs plus regression coverage for output-path errors.

title: Add shared archaeology fixture corpus assets
description:
Context:
Multiple later tasks need the same representative archaeology inputs, but fixture assets should not be bundled with loader code. This issue owns only the checked-in Phase 1 fixture corpus so later tests and workflows can reuse one source of truth without file contention on support code.

Deliverables:
- representative Hyperion-style mixed-source archaeology claim fixtures
- at least one refusal-oriented fixture set and one converging/escalating real-slice-style fixture set
- checked-in policy/input fixture manifest or README that explains each case and its intended outcome surface
- no fixture-loader code or snapshot assertions
acceptance_criteria:
1. The checked-in corpus includes at least one refusal case, one converging case, and one escalating case.
2. Every fixture stays within Phase 1 archaeology boundaries and avoids deferred modes.
3. Fixture assets are organized so later tests and smoke runs can reuse them without duplicating raw inputs.
4. This task does not add loader helpers, snapshot assertions, or CI workflow wiring.
notes:
Target files:
- tests/fixtures/archaeology/**
- tests/fixtures/README.md or equivalent manifest

Reservation guidance: own raw shared fixture assets only; do not add loader code or workflow automation here.

title: Implement logical bucket keys and bucket_id construction
description:
Context:
Bucket identity should not be hidden inside the bucket-store task. This issue owns the structural key model for base buckets and edge buckets so later grouping logic can remain small and deterministic.

Deliverables:
- logical bucket key types for base and edge buckets
- `bucket_id` construction in `sha256:<64 hex>` form from canonical JSON
- explicit edge-property coverage for `reads`, `writes`, `depends_on`, `used_by`, and `authoritative_for`
- focused tests proving base/edge non-collision and stable hashing
acceptance_criteria:
1. Base buckets and edge buckets for the same subject/property never collide.
2. Identical logical bucket keys always hash to the same `bucket_id` across reruns.
3. Every edge property type is covered by at least one bucket-key test.
4. Bucket-key code does not implement grouping, duplicate collapse, or policy behavior.
notes:
Target files:
- src/bucket/key.rs
- tests/buckets/key.rs

Reservation guidance: own bucket identity only; do not absorb store logic or reporting here.

title: Implement shared vocabulary enums and subject/value refs
description:
Context:
Several Phase 1 tasks share the same enum and reference vocabulary. Keeping those types inside the claim-parser bead creates an artificial bottleneck and broader file scope than necessary.

Deliverables:
- shared enums for `source.kind`, `subject.kind`, and `property_type`
- shared subject-ref/value-ref types used across claims, bucket keys, policies, and outputs
- focused tests proving the frozen vocabulary accepts known values and rejects unknown values
acceptance_criteria:
1. Shared vocabulary types compile independently of claim parsing and output rendering logic.
2. Known Phase 1 enum values round-trip through serde as expected.
3. Unknown enum values fail parsing in focused vocabulary tests.
4. This task does not parse full claims, load policy files, or render outputs.
notes:
Target files:
- src/contracts/vocab.rs
- tests/contracts/vocab.rs

Reservation guidance: own shared vocabulary and ref types only; downstream tasks should consume these types rather than redefining them.

title: Ship decoding archaeology v0
description:
Context:
Phase 1 decoding is now intentionally narrow: consume derived claim.v0 from Crucible scan, converge ambiguous archaeology claims into a deterministic canonical map, and emit bounded escalations where ambiguity remains. This epic tracks the minimum implementation needed to make that wedge real.

Deliverables:
- a Rust crate that ships only the archaeology CLI surface
- frozen claim, policy, and output contracts implemented in code
- deterministic bucketing, resolution, and reporting
- fixture, snapshot, CI, and release confidence consistent with other spine primitives
acceptance_criteria:
1. The repo ships a narrow `decoding archaeology` binary and does not implement deferred modes in Phase 1.
2. Derived `claim.v0` plus `legacy.decode.v0.json` always produce byte-stable `canon_entry.v0`, `escalation.v0`, and `convergence.v0` artifacts for identical inputs.
3. The refusal boundary, bucket identity, resolver behavior, and structured outputs are all covered by automated tests and fixture snapshots.
4. One representative Hyperion-style archaeology fixture produces a useful canonical map plus a bounded review queue.
notes:
Tracker only:
- this epic exists to hold the Phase 1 archaeology wedge together
- it is not meant to be the first execution target in `br ready`
- keep child tasks aligned with docs/PLAN_DECODING.md and the Phase 1 boundaries

title: Fix comparator handling for wrapped scalar and string_set claim values
Implement claim.v0 parser and claim refusal boundary
description:
Context:
The decoder only works if the incoming claim contract is executable in code before resolver logic starts drifting. This task owns `claim.v0` parsing, validation, and claim-side refusal behavior, consuming the shared vocabulary types defined elsewhere.

Deliverables:
- `src/contracts/claim.rs`
- parser/validator for derived `claim.v0`
- use of the shared `source.kind`, `subject.kind`, and `property_type` enums
- refusal errors for malformed JSONL, malformed claim IDs, unknown enums, and invalid claim value shapes
acceptance_criteria:
1. Valid claim fixtures parse into typed Rust structures without reopening plan questions.
2. Every claim-side refusal condition named in docs/PLAN_DECODING.md is covered by a focused test and never reaches resolver code.
3. The parser consumes shared vocabulary types instead of redefining enums locally.
4. This task does not define policy loading or output wire formats.
notes:
Target files:
- src/contracts/claim.rs
- tests/contracts/claim.rs

Reservation guidance: own incoming claim parsing and refusal only; shared vocab lives in `src/contracts/vocab.rs`.

title: Fix compare.rs rustfmt regression
Implement canon_entry and escalation wire contract types
description:
Context:
Canon-entry and escalation record contracts belong together because they are both rendered from resolver decisions. The convergence report contract is tracked separately.

Deliverables:
- `src/contracts/canon_entry.rs`
- `src/contracts/escalation.rs`
- serde serialization/deserialization tests for the frozen canon/escalation output shapes
- typed `candidate_values` support for escalation payloads
acceptance_criteria:
1. `canon_entry.v0` and `escalation.v0` types serialize with the frozen field names and shapes.
2. `candidate_values` serialize only as the allowed scalar or subject-ref forms.
3. Round-trip tests pass for representative canon/escalation fixtures.
4. This task does not define the convergence report contract or render output records.
notes:
Target files:
- src/contracts/canon_entry.rs
- src/contracts/escalation.rs
- tests/contracts/canon_escalation.rs

Reservation guidance: own canon/escalation wire types only; do not absorb convergence, rendering, or resolver logic.

title: Implement legacy.decode.v0 policy loader and validator
description:
Context:
The resolver needs a typed, minimal policy surface for auto-resolve, corroboration thresholds, and source priority. This task owns the full `legacy.decode.v0.json` contract, loader, and validator so policy behavior is not split across multiple issues.

Deliverables:
- serde schema for the Phase 1 policy contract
- parser/validator for `legacy.decode.v0.json`
- validation of allowed keys and value shapes
- typed accessors for `auto_resolve`, `min_corroboration`, and `source_priority`
- refusal on unknown policy keys or malformed policy values
acceptance_criteria:
1. A valid `legacy.decode.v0.json` fixture loads into a typed policy object.
2. Unknown policy keys or malformed values fail fast and do not reach resolution code.
3. Auto-resolve, corroboration thresholds, and source-priority data are available to the resolver through typed APIs.
4. Policy-focused tests cover both happy-path and refusal cases.
notes:
Target files:
- src/contracts/policy.rs
- tests/contracts/policy.rs

Reservation guidance: own the policy contract and runtime loader only; do not encode extra property-specific logic here beyond the frozen surface.

title: Generate convergence.v0 summaries from resolver output
description:
Context:
Convergence reporting is a separate concern from record rendering. This issue owns the aggregate `convergence.v0` report surface and the deterministic summary math behind it.

Deliverables:
- generate `convergence.v0` with totals, by-property-type counts, by-source-kind counts, and top escalations
- deterministic ordering for top-escalation entries
- focused tests for convergence totals and summary breakdowns
acceptance_criteria:
1. `convergence.v0` output conforms to the frozen wire contract.
2. Report totals, by-property-type counts, and by-source-kind counts are correct for representative fixtures.
3. Top escalations are ordered deterministically for identical inputs.
4. Report generation does not render canonical/escalation records directly.
notes:
Target files:
- src/report.rs
- tests/report.rs

Reservation guidance: own convergence summaries only; do not render canon/escalation records or modify resolver rules here.

title: Implement convergence.v0 wire contract type
description:
Context:
The convergence report contract should not share a bead with canon/escalation record types. This issue owns only the `convergence.v0` wire type used by the reporting layer.

Deliverables:
- `src/contracts/convergence.rs`
- serde serialization/deserialization tests for the frozen `convergence.v0` shape
- any shared helper types needed only by the convergence output contract
acceptance_criteria:
1. `convergence.v0` serializes with the frozen field names and shapes.
2. Representative round-trip tests pass for the report contract.
3. This task does not render reports or serialize canon/escalation records.
4. This task stays limited to the convergence output surface.
notes:
Target files:
- src/contracts/convergence.rs
- tests/contracts/convergence.rs

Reservation guidance: own the convergence contract only; do not absorb renderer or report logic here.

title: Fix comparator decoding for wrapped claim values
description: The compare registry currently treats semantic_label, liveness, and valid_values as raw strings/arrays, but claim.v0 wraps them as {kind,value} / {kind,values}. This causes false incompatibility and would force incorrect escalations in the resolver. Fix compare.rs to decode the frozen claim shapes and add focused tests using real fixture-style values.

title: Implement normalization and canonical hashing helpers
description:
Context:
Deterministic convergence depends on one normalization layer. This issue owns canonical JSON, normalized string/set helpers, and hashing utilities used by later bucket and output code.

Deliverables:
- canonical JSON helpers
- normalized string and sorted-set helpers
- stable hash helpers for content-addressed IDs
acceptance_criteria:
1. Canonical JSON rendering is stable across reruns for identical logical inputs.
2. Normalized string and sorted-set helpers are deterministic.
3. Hash helpers always emit `sha256:<64 hex>` in lowercase.
4. This task does not depend on claim parsing or define bucket keys/grouping behavior.
notes:
Target files:
- src/normalize.rs
- tests/normalize.rs

Reservation guidance: own normalization and hashing only; this task should be able to proceed immediately after bootstrap.

title: Lock rendered outputs with snapshot tests
description:
Context:
Once renderers and reporters exist, we need byte-stable snapshots for the three frozen output surfaces. This issue owns snapshot locking only.

Deliverables:
- snapshot assertions for `canon_entry.v0`
- snapshot assertions for `escalation.v0`
- snapshot assertions for `convergence.v0`
acceptance_criteria:
1. Snapshot tests lock stable `canon_entry.v0`, `escalation.v0`, and `convergence.v0` rendering.
2. Snapshot fixtures use the shared archaeology corpus rather than ad hoc inline data where practical.
3. Snapshot drift is visible in test output and requires explicit review.
4. This task does not own CI workflow wiring.
notes:
Target files:
- tests/snapshots/**

Reservation guidance: own snapshot locking only; do not add smoke workflow or runtime metrics here.

title: Implement bucket store, duplicate collapse, and source counting
description:
Context:
Once bucket identity exists, decoding needs a deterministic store that groups claims, collapses duplicate claim IDs, and tracks distinct source artifacts for convergence.

Deliverables:
- grouping by logical bucket key
- duplicate identical `claim_id` collapse before counting
- canonical claim ordering within each bucket
- distinct source-artifact counting
acceptance_criteria:
1. Repeated identical `claim_id`s collapse to one logical claim before bucket counts are computed.
2. Claim ordering inside a bucket is deterministic and independent of input order.
3. `source_count` counts distinct source artifacts, not raw claims.
4. This task does not compute resolver decisions or render reports.
notes:
Target files:
- src/bucket/store.rs
- tests/buckets/store.rs

Reservation guidance: own grouping/counting only; do not absorb resolver or report generation here.

title: Implement resolver engine and decision records
description:
Context:
The core of Phase 1 is the decision engine: take deterministic buckets plus property-aware compatibility and policy, then decide whether a bucket converges or escalates.

Deliverables:
- resolver/state-machine implementation for `single_source`, `converging`, `converged`, `conflicted`, and `escalated`
- deterministic decision records that capture canonical-vs-escalation outcomes
- structured explanation data with winner and compatible claim IDs
- recommended-action and reason assignment for escalations
acceptance_criteria:
1. Resolver fixtures cover all declared bucket states and produce deterministic decision records.
2. Every `resolution_kind` and every `escalation.reason` is exercised by tests.
3. Decision records preserve sorted claim IDs and typed candidate values for later rendering.
4. This task does not serialize wire outputs directly.
notes:
Target files:
- src/resolve/engine.rs
- tests/resolver.rs

Reservation guidance: own decision-making only; do not fold rendering/reporting into the resolver.

title: Bootstrap decoding Rust crate and archaeology CLI shell
description:
Context:
The repo is docs-only today. Before feature work can land safely, decoding needs one initial bootstrap pass that lays down the crate, binary entrypoint, and zero-logic module/file scaffold so later implementation tasks can reserve one leaf file at a time instead of fighting over shared module manifests.

Deliverables:
- Cargo crate bootstrap with release profile and `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`
- `src/lib.rs`, `src/main.rs`, and `src/cli.rs` shell for `decoding archaeology` only
- zero-logic module skeleton matching the plan split (`contracts`, `normalize`, `bucket`, `compare`, `resolve`, `render`, `report`)
- placeholder leaf files and module declarations for the planned Phase 1 child beads so downstream work can stay file-local
- pinned core dependencies needed for Phase 1 code work
acceptance_criteria:
1. The crate compiles on a fresh clone.
2. `cargo run -- archaeology --help` shows the declared Phase 1 CLI surface and no deferred modes.
3. The crate uses the repo standard release profile, forbids unsafe code, and exposes the planned module boundaries and placeholder child files in source.
4. No feature logic, CI/release workflow logic, or deferred modes are implemented in this task.
notes:
Target files:
- Cargo.toml
- Cargo.lock
- src/lib.rs
- src/main.rs
- src/cli.rs
- src/contracts/mod.rs
- src/contracts/vocab.rs
- src/contracts/claim.rs
- src/contracts/canon_entry.rs
- src/contracts/escalation.rs
- src/contracts/convergence.rs
- src/contracts/policy.rs
- src/normalize.rs
- src/bucket/mod.rs
- src/bucket/key.rs
- src/bucket/store.rs
- src/compare.rs
- src/resolve/mod.rs
- src/resolve/engine.rs
- src/render.rs
- src/report.rs

Reservation guidance: this is the one intentionally broader scaffold task; its job is to create zero-logic leaf files so later beads can reserve one or two files each.
close_reason: Scaffolded Rust crate: Cargo.toml with release profile, forbid(unsafe_code), clap/serde/sha2/indexmap/insta deps. CLI shell with archaeology subcommand matching plan spec. Zero-logic module skeleton: contracts/ (claim, canon_entry, escalation, convergence, policy, vocabulary), normalize, bucket, compare, resolve, render, report. All todo!() stubs with correct signatures. Compiles clean on fmt + clippy + check.

title: Implement property comparator registry and liveness fold
description:
Context:
Phase 1 deliberately keeps a small frozen property vocabulary. Each property type needs one explicit compatibility rule so resolution remains deterministic and reviewable instead of hiding behind a catch-all comparator.

Deliverables:
- comparator registry keyed by frozen `property_type`
- compatibility/incompatibility logic for every Phase 1 property type
- isolated liveness fold behavior for `alive`, `stale`, `unknown`, and conflict cases
- test corpus proving the registry covers the whole vocabulary
acceptance_criteria:
1. Every frozen `property_type` has an explicit comparator implementation.
2. Compatibility and incompatibility cases exist for every comparator.
3. Liveness accepts `alive + stale` and `stale + unknown`, and rejects `alive + dead` as a conflict.
4. No fallback catch-all comparator exists for unknown property types.
notes:
Target files:
- src/compare.rs
- tests/comparators.rs

Reservation guidance: own compatibility logic only; do not absorb bucket grouping or policy loading here.

title: Add fast CI quality-gate workflow with cargo caching
description:
Context:
We need regression protection without bloating the default pipeline. This issue owns the fast workflow that enforces the Rust quality gate, uses caching to keep runtime bounded, and emits lightweight timing visibility for the fast path.

Deliverables:
- workflow running `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test`
- cargo dependency/build caching
- workflow separation that keeps slower smoke coverage out of the fast path
- job-summary or artifact output recording fast-path elapsed time
acceptance_criteria:
1. Pushes and pull requests run the declared Rust quality gate in CI.
2. Cargo caches are configured so repeated runs avoid unnecessary cold-start work.
3. The fast workflow does not include the slower end-to-end smoke path.
4. The workflow emits lightweight elapsed-time visibility for the fast path so regressions can be spotted over time.
notes:
Target files:
- .github/workflows/ci.yml

Reservation guidance: own the fast CI path only; do not add tagged release logic or smoke coverage here.

title: Render canon_entry and escalation records from decision outputs
description:
Context:
Decision records from the resolver should be rendered to wire artifacts by a dedicated layer rather than by the resolver or CLI. This issue owns canonical-entry and escalation rendering only.

Deliverables:
- render canonical decisions into `canon_entry.v0` records
- render escalation decisions into `escalation.v0` records
- deterministic ordering of claim IDs and candidate values in rendered output
- focused renderer tests for stable JSON/JSONL output
acceptance_criteria:
1. Rendered `canon_entry.v0` and `escalation.v0` records conform to the frozen wire contracts.
2. Repeated renders of identical decision inputs produce byte-stable JSON/JSONL outputs.
3. Claim IDs and candidate values are sorted deterministically in rendered records.
4. Rendering does not introduce new policy or state-transition logic.
notes:
Target files:
- src/render.rs
- tests/render.rs

Reservation guidance: own canon/escalation rendering only; do not generate convergence reports or alter resolver behavior here.

title: Make policy validation deterministic
description: Policy validation iterates HashMap-backed keyed sections in randomized order. For invalid policies with multiple bad entries, the refusal reason can vary across runs, breaking deterministic Phase 1 refusal behavior. Fix by using deterministic keyed collections and add coverage.

title: Fix global duplicate claim_id collapse across buckets
Wire archaeology CLI file loading, execution, and exit codes
description:
Context:
After the runtime can produce rendered records and convergence reports, the binary needs to load multiple claim files, apply one policy file, invoke the runtime, write output artifacts, and map outcomes to the Phase 1 exit codes.

Deliverables:
- CLI file-loading orchestration for multiple claim JSONL inputs
- runtime invocation over one policy file plus the loaded claims
- writing canon-entry, escalation, and convergence outputs to requested files
- exit-code mapping (`0` no escalations, `1` escalations emitted, `2` refusal)
- machine-readable `--json` status/error output
acceptance_criteria:
1. The CLI accepts multiple claim JSONL files plus one policy file and writes all declared output artifacts.
2. Exit code `0` means no escalations, `1` means escalations were emitted, and `2` means refusal/invalid input.
3. Re-running the CLI on identical inputs produces byte-for-byte identical output files.
4. The CLI uses the runtime/render/report layers rather than re-serializing outputs ad hoc.
notes:
Target files:
- src/cli.rs
- src/main.rs
- tests/cli.rs

Reservation guidance: own top-level orchestration and exit-code behavior only; do not re-implement contracts, resolver logic, or report math here.

title: Add tagged release workflow for decoding binaries
description:
Context:
Release publication should be separate from the fast CI workflow. This issue owns the tagged workflow that builds release binaries once the CLI surface is in place.

Deliverables:
- tagged release workflow building binaries for the declared supported platforms
- workflow wiring that publishes only on tags or explicit release triggers
- release artifact naming consistent with other spine primitives
acceptance_criteria:
1. Tagged releases build binaries for the supported platforms declared for v0.
2. The release workflow is separate from the fast CI workflow.
3. Release artifacts use stable, documented names.
4. The workflow does not publish on every push or pull request.
notes:
Target files:
- .github/workflows/release.yml

Reservation guidance: own tagged release automation only; do not change the fast quality gate here.
